title: Linguistic Depth, Score Reliability & Graph Topology
rationale: Phase 15 added recovery intelligence, expanded biases (4→9), metacognitive monitoring, adaptive learning rates, and Bayesian changepoint detection. Web research + codebase gap analysis reveals 3 critical gaps: (1) ConversationProcessor uses keyword matching but has no readability metrics (Flesch-Kincaid, SMOG) — cognitive clarity is a direct proxy for trader state. (2) No temporal linguistic tracking — trader writing style changes (pronoun shifts, sentence volatility, vocabulary diversity) are early warning signals of deterioration. (3) No Cronbach's alpha or internal consistency measurement — 6 readiness components may be multicollinear or noisy, and we can't tell. Additionally: (4) Graph topology is hand-crafted (4 features) but misses principled graph statistics (clustering, centrality, community detection). (5) BiasDetector doesn't use US-280 negation-awareness — 'I'm NOT overconfident' triggers overconfidence detection incorrectly. Phase 16 focuses on deepening semantic analysis, measuring score reliability, and enriching graph-derived features.

stories.title: Text readability metrics for cognitive state estimation
stories.description: Add Flesch-Kincaid reading ease, Gunning Fog index, and vocabulary diversity to ConversationSignals. Research shows cognitive state correlates with text complexity: stressed/impulsive traders write shorter, simpler sentences; disciplined traders write longer, more structured text. Integrate textstat library for standardized readability scoring. Add readability_score to ConversationSignals and blend into cognitive_load computation at 30% weight (current text-structure features at 70%).
acceptance: pip install textstat (lightweight, no heavy deps)
ConversationProcessor.process_message() computes 3 readability metrics: flesch_reading_ease, gunning_fog, vocabulary_diversity (unique_words / total_words)
ConversationSignals gains readability_score: float field (0-1 normalized)
Normalization: flesch_reading_ease mapped from 0-100 to 0-1; gunning_fog inverted and capped; vocabulary_diversity direct
readability_score = 0.4 * flesch_norm + 0.3 * fog_norm + 0.3 * vocab_diversity
cognitive_load computation updated: 0.70 * text_structure_load + 0.30 * (1 - readability_score)
Short messages (< 5 words) return readability_score = 0.5 (neutral, insufficient text)

At least 8 tests: simple text high readability, complex text low readability, short text neutral, vocabulary diversity scales, integration with cognitive_load, flesch normalization bounds, fog normalization bounds, empty text safe

stories.title: Temporal linguistic style tracking for early warning
stories.description: Track 5 linguistic style metrics across consecutive messages to detect deterioration: (1) avg_sentence_length — drops under stress. (2) exclamation_density — spikes with emotional escalation. (3) caps_ratio — rises with anger/frustration. (4) pronoun_i_ratio — shifts indicate distancing or hyper-focus. (5) question_ratio — increases with uncertainty. Compute a style_drift_score comparing current message's 5-vector against the rolling 10-message baseline (Euclidean distance). High drift = potential state change. Store style history in ConversationProcessor for cross-message tracking.
acceptance: New class LinguisticStyleTracker in src/aura/analysis/style_tracker.py
track_message(message_text) → StyleSnapshot with 5 metrics
StyleSnapshot: avg_sentence_length, exclamation_density, caps_ratio, pronoun_i_ratio, question_ratio
compute_drift() → float (0-1): Euclidean distance from rolling 10-message baseline, normalized
Baseline starts after 3 messages (returns 0.0 before that)
Rolling window stores last 20 StyleSnapshot entries (FIFO)
ConversationProcessor.process_message() calls style_tracker.track_message() and stores drift score
ConversationSignals gains style_drift_score: float field (0-1)
Drift > 0.5 logged as warning for pattern detection input
At least 8 tests: baseline initialization, normal text low drift, stressed text high drift, caps detection, exclamation detection, question ratio, pronoun shift, rolling window FIFO behavior

stories.title: Cronbach's alpha for readiness score reliability
stories.description: Measure internal consistency of the 6 readiness components using Cronbach's alpha over a sliding window. If alpha < 0.6, the readiness score is noisy (components disagree) — flag this in the signal so Buddy can discount it. Also compute split-half reliability by comparing odd/even component pairs. Add reliability_score to ReadinessSignal. If reliability < 0.5 and readiness > 60, add a warning: 'high readiness but low consistency — treat with caution'.
acceptance: New class ReadinessReliabilityAnalyzer in src/aura/analysis/reliability.py
record_components(components_dict: Dict[str, float]) stores component vectors
cronbachs_alpha(window: int = 20) → float: computes alpha over last N component snapshots
split_half_reliability() → float: correlation between odd-indexed and even-indexed component averages
reliability_score = 0.6 * cronbachs_alpha + 0.4 * split_half_reliability
Minimum 10 snapshots before scoring activates (returns 0.7 default below threshold)
ReadinessComputer.compute() records components and computes reliability_score
ReadinessSignal gains reliability_score: float field (0-1)
ReadinessSignal.to_dict() includes reliability_score

At least 8 tests: insufficient data returns default, perfect consistency alpha=1.0, random components low alpha, split-half correlation, reliability in signal, window sliding works, warning on low reliability + high readiness, integration with compute()

stories.title: Graph topology statistics for structural readiness features
stories.description: Replace hand-crafted graph features (US-320) with principled graph topology statistics using networkx algorithms. Compute 6 features: (1) clustering_coefficient — triangles in trader's goal/decision network. (2) avg_betweenness — bottleneck identification. (3) density — graph connectivity. (4) num_communities — Louvain community detection for life-domain fragmentation. (5) modularity — how separated communities are. (6) largest_component_ratio — largest connected component / total nodes. These replace the 4 hand-crafted features and feed into graph_context_score at the same 10% weight.
acceptance: New class GraphTopologyAnalyzer in src/aura/analysis/graph_topology.py
analyze(graph: SelfModelGraph) → GraphTopologyFeatures dataclass with 6 float fields
Uses networkx for computation: build nx.Graph from SelfModelGraph edges
clustering_coefficient: nx.average_clustering(G), default 0.0 if < 3 nodes
avg_betweenness: mean(nx.betweenness_centrality(G).values()), default 0.0
density: nx.density(G), default 0.0
num_communities: len(nx.community.louvain_communities(G, seed=42)), normalized to 0-1 via min(communities/10, 1.0)
modularity: nx.community.modularity(G, communities), clamped 0-1
largest_component_ratio: max(len(c) for c in nx.connected_components(G)) / G.number_of_nodes()
graph_context_score = mean of 6 features (each 0-1)
ReadinessComputer uses GraphTopologyAnalyzer instead of hand-crafted _compute_graph_features()
Graceful fallback: if networkx unavailable or graph empty, return all 0.5 (neutral)

At least 8 tests: empty graph neutral, single node safe, connected graph high density, community detection works, clustering coefficient, betweenness centrality, modularity score, integration with readiness compute

stories.title: Negation-aware bias detection
stories.description: BiasDetector (US-327) uses exact keyword matching without negation awareness — 'I am NOT overconfident' triggers overconfidence=true. Apply the same negation logic from US-280 (ConversationProcessor._apply_negation_awareness) to all 9 bias detection methods. Check for negation words ('not', 'no', 'never', 'don\'t', 'didn\'t', 'isn\'t', 'won\'t', 'can\'t', 'haven\'t', 'shouldn\'t') within 3 tokens before a bias keyword. If negated, skip the match. This is a targeted correctness fix that prevents false positive bias detection.
acceptance: BiasDetector gains _is_negated(text, keyword_position) helper method
Negation check: scan 3 tokens before keyword for negation words
NEGATION_WORDS list: 'not', 'no', 'never', 'don't', 'didn't', 'isn't', 'won't', 'can't', 'haven't', 'shouldn't', 'neither', 'nor', 'without'
All 9 bias detection methods (disposition, loss_aversion, recency, confirmation, sunk_cost, anchoring, overconfidence, hindsight, attribution_error) pass keywords through negation filter
Negated matches excluded from bias score computation
Non-negated matches still score identically to previous behavior

At least 8 tests: negated overconfidence not detected, non-negated overconfidence still detected, negated sunk cost not detected, double negation handling, negation window (3 tokens), all 9 biases negation-aware, edge case empty text, edge case keyword at start of text

stories.title: Phase 16 integration tests + /reliability and /style CLI commands
stories.description: Write integration tests verifying the full Phase 16 pipeline: (1) readability metrics flow through cognitive_load to readiness. (2) Style drift detected across message sequence. (3) Cronbach's alpha computed from component history. (4) Graph topology features replace hand-crafted. (5) Negated biases correctly excluded. Plus add /reliability CLI command showing Cronbach's alpha, split-half, and reliability_score. Add /style CLI command showing last 5 style snapshots with drift indicator.
acceptance: At least 3 integration tests using real instances (no mocks on production code)
Test 1: Complex message → readability_score < 0.4 → cognitive_load increases → readiness drops
Test 2: Sequence of 5 calm messages then 5 stressed messages → style_drift_score > 0.3
Test 3: 20 compute() calls → cronbachs_alpha computed → reliability_score in signal
Test 4: Graph with nodes → topology features computed → graph_context_score in readiness
Test 5: Negated bias text → bias score = 0.0 for negated keyword, non-negated still detected
AuraCompanion handles /reliability command: displays alpha, split-half, composite with visual bars
AuraCompanion handles /style command: displays last 5 style snapshots with drift arrows
All integration tests use NEUTRAL_CIRCADIAN to isolate from time-of-day effects
At least 8 tests total across integration + CLI scenarios
